title: Can't create HyperV podman machine although being part of HyperV admin group
body:
### Issue Description

Tried podman machine init with CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=hyperv and got:

```
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.0 to create VM
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4918dae5ef6d done   |
Copying config 44136fa355 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
4918dae5ef6d941322860602899cc4d12ba4e086860527a231b1ae21bb4110c2
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-amd64.vhdx: done
time="2024-03-20T19:38:54+01:00" level=error msg="Descripteur non valide"
Error: Accès refusé.
```

I am a member of the HyperV admin group:

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/695993/d3b624e7-a2bf-4534-8309-16a30f8f976a)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. set CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=hyperv
2. podman machine init
3.


### Describe the results you received

```
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.0 to create VM
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4918dae5ef6d done   |
Copying config 44136fa355 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
4918dae5ef6d941322860602899cc4d12ba4e086860527a231b1ae21bb4110c2
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-amd64.vhdx: done
time="2024-03-20T19:38:54+01:00" level=error msg="Descripteur non valide"
Error: Accès refusé.
```


### Describe the results you expected

machine should be created

### podman info output

```yaml
Win11Pro
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @n1hility does this make you think that something we did in libhvee is actually requiring admin?
the admin is requirement for the hvsock.  looking at possible ways to deal with this.
#22161 will require admin now ... perhaps for 5.1 we can work around the hvsock requirement. we have a number of ideas.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am curious if we have any update on the issue?

As commented [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23578#issuecomment-3585120040) it's now possible to start/stop hyper-v machines if a user belong to the hyperv administrators. The commands `machine init` and `machine rm` require to be run in elevated mode to add/remove the entries in the Windows registry but subsequent machines init/rm don't need it (the same registry key is shared among machines).

Closing this issue: after https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28522, if an admin configures the host (running `podman system hyperv-prep`) machines can be created, started and deleted from a non-elevated terminal. The fix is currently in the main branch and will be in the release v6.0
